zh.County Tyrone, Northern Ireland, United: 英國北愛爾蘭庫迪 泰龍
zh.lifeSummary: 當Niall Mór "The Big" O'Neill - of Tyrone於大約1340年出生在愛爾蘭武爾斯特時，他的父親Hugh "the Fat" O'Neill, King of Ulster and Tyrone52歲，母親Gormflaith O'Donnell of Tir Conaill25歲。他於大約1359年與Benmee MacMahon在愛爾蘭武爾斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於從1397年1月9日到1398年1月8日。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Niall Mór "The Big" O'Neill - of Tyrone于大约1340年出生在爱尔兰武爾斯特时，他的父亲Hugh "the Fat" O'Neill, King of Ulster and Tyrone52岁，母亲Gormflaith O'Donnell of Tir Conaill25岁。他于大约1359年与Benmee MacMahon在爱尔兰武爾斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于从1397年1月9日到1398年1月8日。

nameMeanings.name: O'Neill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan, Dermot, Liam, Nuala, Bridie, Brigid, Conor, Fintan, John Patrick.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Néill ‘descendant of Niall’, an Irish personal name possibly derived from néal ‘cloud’ (see Neil ).

History: Bearers of this surname mostly claim descent from Niall Noígíallach (Niall of the Nine Hostages), a legendary king of Ireland who flourished in the 5th century. His descendants, the Ui Neill dynasty, dominated Ireland, on and off, from the 6th to the 10th century. The surname itself can be traced with some confidence to one of these descendants, namely Niall Glúndubh mac Aodha (died 919), High King of Ireland from 911 to 919, with a power base in Tyrone, Ulster.

variants.name: O'Neall
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: McNelis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Brendan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Niallghuis ‘son of Niallghus’, a personal name (intended as complimentary) composed of the personal name Niall (see O'Neill ) + gus ‘vigor’. In Irish the combination of [k] and [n] was generally pronounced [kr], Anglicized as the variants McCreless and McCrillis .

variants.name: O'Neal
meanings: Irish (Longford, Down): variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: O'Niel
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Odneal
meanings: Perhaps an irregular variant of Irish   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Nealon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Declan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Nialláin ‘descendant of Niallán’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of Niall, see O'Neill , Neil .

variants.name: O'Neil
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Conn, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Aileen, Eamon, Michael Patrick, Seamus, Sean Patrick, William Sean.
Irish (Mayo, Tipperary): variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Neil
meanings: Scottish, English (Lancashire), and Irish (Munster): from the Gaelic personal name Niall (genitive Néill), possibly derived from Irish néal ‘cloud’. In most cases the name probably represents a shortened form of the derived patronymics McNeill or the comparable Irish O'Neill .
English: variant of   Neal  .

variants.name: O'Neel
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

nameMeanings.name: Niall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of disputed derivation; it may mean ‘cloud’, ‘passionate’, or perhaps ‘champion’. It was adopted by the Scandinavians in the form Njal and soon became very popular among them. From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, it has been strongly revived among non-Gaelic speakers since the latter part of the 20th century.

variants.name: Neal
meanings: Variant spelling of   Neil .

variants.name: Neale
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

variants.name: Neil
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

nameMeanings.name: Mór
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from mór ‘large, great’. This was the commonest of all girls’ names in late medieval Ireland, and has continued in use in both Ireland and Scotland to the present day.

nameMeanings.name: "The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Big"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on (or just composed of) a word meaning ‘big’, such as Navajo Hastiin-tsoh ‘Big Man’ (see Tso , compare Bigman ).

Probably an altered or shortened form of some English surname such as   Bigg   and   Biggs  , or an English translation or adaptation of some cognate or like-sounding name from continental Europe (compare   Bigg   3).

variants.name: Tso
meanings: ‘Great waves’.

en.lifeSummary: When Niall Mór "The Big" O'Neill - of Tyrone was born about 1340, in Ulster, Ireland, his father, Hugh "the Fat" O'Neill, King of Ulster and Tyrone, was 52 and his mother, Gormflaith O'Donnell of Tir Conaill, was 25. He married Benmee MacMahon about 1359, in Ulster, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died from 9 January 1397 to 8 January 1398.

fr.County Tyrone, Northern Ireland, United: County Tyrone, Irlande du Nord, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Niall Mór "The Big" O'Neill - of Tyrone est né environ 1340, à Ulster, Irlande, son père, Hugh "the Fat" O'Neill, King of Ulster and Tyrone, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Gormflaith O'Donnell of Tir Conaill, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Benmee MacMahon environ 1359, à Ulster, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé du 9 janvier 1397 au 8 janvier 1398.

de.lifeSummary: Als Niall Mór "The Big" O'Neill - of Tyrone um 1340 in Ulster, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh "the Fat" O'Neill, King of Ulster and Tyrone 52 und seine Mutter Gormflaith O'Donnell of Tir Conaill 25. Er hat um 1359 in Ulster, Irland Benmee MacMahon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb von 9. Januar 1397 bis 8. Januar 1398.

ko.lifeSummary: Niall Mór "The Big" O'Neill - of Tyrone(이)가 대략 1340년 아일랜드 울스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh "the Fat" O'Neill, King of Ulster and Tyrone(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Gormflaith O'Donnell of Tir Conaill(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1359년 아일랜드 울스터에서 Benmee MacMahon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1397년 1월 9일부터 1398년 1월 8일까지 사망했다.
